title: 5-Day Training Course on "The Application of Geo-Synthetics in Road Infrastructure Projects"
content: Event Date: 7th April 2025 – 11th April 2025 Event brief description This 5-day training course, organized by Galgotias University, in collaboration with CSIR-CRRI, focused on the applications of geo-synthetics in road infrastructure projects. The event brought together experts, faculty, and students to discuss the latest advancements in the field, offering a comprehensive understanding of geo-synthetics’ role in enhancing road construction. The course aimed to provide participants with practical knowledge and insights on how to use geo-synthetics to improve the durability and efficiency of road infrastructure projects. Participants had the opportunity to attend lectures, engage in hands-on training, and learn from industry experts. Topics included the use of geo-textiles, geo-membranes, and geo-grids in road construction, as well as the environmental benefits of these materials. The course was well-received, with attendees coming from various institutions and industries. Event Detailed Description The 5-day training course on "The Application of Geo-Synthetics in Road Infrastructure Projects" aimed to educate participants about the growing importance of geo-synthetics in modern civil engineering. Held at Galgotias University from 7th to 11th April 2025, the event was a joint effort by the Department of Civil Engineering, Galgotias University, CSIR-CRRI, and the Ministry of Textiles. This training course provided a blend of theory and practical sessions. Expert speakers from the field of geo-synthetics shared their knowledge and real-world experiences in using these materials in road projects. Topics included the various types of geo-synthetics, their uses in drainage systems, erosion control, and the reinforcement of soil in road construction. Participants were also exposed to case studies showcasing the successful application of geo-synthetics in Indian road projects. A significant aspect of the event was the hands-on experience provided to participants, allowing them to work with geo-synthetic materials. This practical approach, coupled with the technical lectures, gave participants the skills and knowledge to incorporate geo-synthetics into their own projects. The training was conducted in a collaborative environment, where both students and professionals from the civil engineering field interacted and shared insights. The event concluded with a certificate distribution ceremony, followed by a networking session where attendees could discuss potential collaborations. The training course received positive feedback from all participants, who praised the depth of knowledge shared and the practical applications of the materials discussed. Department Name –Civil Engineering, Galgotias University Event Outcome The 5-day training course successfully imparted knowledge on the applications of geo-synthetics in road infrastructure projects.
